title: Introduction to Angular
content:
### What is Angular?

Angular is a **TypeScript-based open-source web application framework** developed and maintained by Google. It is a complete rewrite of AngularJS (1.x) and is designed for building dynamic, large-scale single-page applications (SPAs).

### Key Features

- **Component Architecture** — The UI is built from a tree of components, each with its own template, styles, and logic. Components are the fundamental building blocks.
- **Modules (NgModules)** — Angular organizes code into modules using the `@NgModule` decorator. The root module (`AppModule`) bootstraps the application. Feature modules encapsulate related functionality.
- **Dependency Injection (DI)** — Angular has a built-in DI system that makes services available across the application without manual instantiation.
- **RxJS (Reactive Extensions)** — Angular uses RxJS Observables extensively for handling asynchronous operations like HTTP requests, form value changes, and event streams.
- **CLI Tools** — The Angular CLI (`ng`) provides scaffolding, build optimization, testing, and deployment commands out of the box.

### Angular vs React vs Vue

| Feature | Angular | React | Vue |
|---------|---------|-------|-----|
| Type | Full framework | Library (UI) | Progressive framework |
| Language | TypeScript | JavaScript/TSX | JavaScript/HTML |
| Data Binding | Two-way (banana box) | One-way (unidirectional) | Two-way |
| State Management | Services + RxJS | useState / Redux | Pinia / Vuex |
| Routing | Built-in `@angular/router` | react-router (external) | vue-router (official) |
| Forms | Template-driven & Reactive | Manual state | v-model |
| DI | Built-in | Manual / Context | Provide / Inject |
| Learning Curve | Steep | Moderate | Gentle |

Angular provides a **batteries-included** experience with built-in routing, HTTP client, forms, and testing utilities — whereas React requires piecing together libraries from the ecosystem.
